Find the cost of 1\,m of fabric.

tex:
**Without using a calculator**, work out $\displaystyle\frac{5}{9} - \frac{1}{6}$.

You must show all your working and give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.

Daryl records the number of hours in a week 8 people spend exercising.

5 \qquad 2 \qquad 1.5 \qquad 3 \qquad 18 \qquad 4.5 \qquad 2 \qquad 4
(a) 
t Find the median.

t Explain why the mean may not be a suitable average to use.

The diagram shows three triangles A, B and C.

(a) 
t Which two of the triangles A, B and C are congruent with each other?

t Draw a ring around the congruence criterion that can be used to support your answer to part (a).

SSS \qquad\qquad ASA \qquad\qquad SAS \qquad\qquad RHS

Calculate.

(a) 
t $2000 \times 1.2^3$

t $\displaystyle 2\frac{1}{8} \times \frac{6}{17}$

t $\displaystyle\frac{4.5(\cos 30°)^2}{\sqrt{3}} - 2$

The graph of $y = (x - 3)(x + b)(x + 2)$ intersects the $y$-axis at $-30$.

(a) 
t Find the value of $b$.

t When $x > 0$ the graph crosses the $x$-axis once.

Write down the coordinates of this point.

{---} ,  )][]

$x = 3^2 \times 5^2 \times 7 \times 199^{57}$ when written as a product of its prime factors.

Write $x \div 315$ as a product of its prime factors.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($k$\,kg) & Cumulative Frequency \\
\hline
$k \leqslant 20$ & 0 \\
\hline
$k \leqslant 22$ & 7 \\
\hline

\hline
$k \leqslant 28$ & 35 \\
\hline
$k \leqslant 32$ & 43 \\
\hline
$k \leqslant 36$ & 47 \\
\hline
$k \leqslant 42$ & 50 \\

t Draw a cumulative frequency diagram to show this information.

t Use your graph to find an estimate of the 90th percentile.

tex:
136 girls and 144 boys each measure the distance they jump in centimetres.

The box-and-whisker plots show the distributions of these distances.

Each child who jumps a distance greater than 160\,cm gets a certificate.

Work out an estimate of the total number of children who get a certificate.

The diagram shows a quadrilateral.

Give a geometrical reason why this is a cyclic quadrilateral.

(a) 
t Shade the region indicated in each Venn diagram.

t The Venn diagram shows some information about the number of elements in sets $A$, $B$, $C$ and $\mathscr{E}$.

Given the following information, complete the Venn diagram.

$\text{n}(A \cap B \cap C) = 1$

$\text{n}(A \cup B \cup C)' = 17$

$\text{n}(C) = 42$

$\text{f}(x) = x^2$ \qquad\qquad $\text{g}(x) = \displaystyle\frac{x + 5}{2}$ \qquad\qquad $\text{h}(x) = 7x - 3$
(a) 
t Find $\text{f}(-3)$.

t Find $\text{g}^{-1}(x)$.

t Solve $\text{gf}(x) = \text{hh}^{-1}(63)$ where $x > 0$.

Write $0.4\dot{1}\dot{9}$ as a fraction in its simplest form.

You must show all your working.

Katy picks a number at random from the numbers 2, 3 and 5.

She then picks a number at random from the numbers 5, 6, 7 and 9.

When she adds the two numbers the answer is even.

Find the probability that exactly one of the numbers picked is a 5.

Simplify fully.

(a) 
t $(81x^{16})^{\frac{3}{4}}$

t $\displaystyle\left(\frac{1}{y^2}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2}}$

A solid is made by cutting a small cone from a larger cone, as shown in the diagram.

The height of the larger cone is 12.5\,cm.

The height of the solid is 5.5\,cm.

The diameter of the base of the larger cone is 9.2\,cm.

Work out the volume of the solid.

[The volume, $V$, of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \displaystyle\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.]

The volumes of two mathematically similar objects are 56\,cm$^3$ and 875\,cm$^3$.

The height of the smaller object is 18\,cm.

Find the height of the larger object.

Solve $\displaystyle\frac{4}{x + 1} + \frac{2}{2x - 5} = 3$.

You must show all your working.
